text: When working with databases, you have two main types: SQL and NoSQL. SQL databases use tables with structured rows and columns, where data relationships are clearly defined. NoSQL databases are more flexible and can store data as documents, graphs, or key-value pairs. To access information, you use queries—commands that retrieve, update, or delete data. Before storing data, careful data modeling is essential to organize it efficiently and avoid redundancy. Additionally, indexes are special structures that speed up database queries by allowing faster data retrieval. Choosing between SQL and NoSQL depends on your project's needs and how you plan to manage the data.
